steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a short vertical line for the butterfly's body. Both wings will balance around this center line.

steps.title: Block the wings (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a larger rounded shape on each side of the axis for the upper wings, and a smaller rounded shape below each for the lower wings. Keep them mirrored across the center line.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the wings
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace each wing's outline in one confident pass, letting the line curve and scallop slightly at the outer edge. The upper wings can be more pointed; the lower wings rounder.

steps.title: Ink the body and antennae (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw a thin oval body along the axis line, slightly pinched at the head end. Add two thin curving antennae rising from the top, each ending in a tiny dot.

steps.title: Suggest the wing pattern (ink)
steps.description: On each wing, add a small loop or two and a scatter of dots — a few marks suggest the pattern. Resist drawing every spot; the viewer fills in the rest.
